title: Seeing Entities During Kundalini Crisis: How to Work With What Appears

body:
Seeing Entities During Kundalini Crisis: How to Work With What Appears

You're here because you're scared. You're seeing things, feeling things, things that aren't supposed to be there. You're awake at 3 AM, heart pounding, sweat cold on your skin, wondering if you're losing your mind. I know that feeling. I've been there. I've seen the shadows, felt the presences, heard the whispers. It's terrifying. It's isolating. And you're probably thinking, "What the hell is happening to me?"

Look, you're not alone. What you're experiencing - these entities, these visions, these phenomena - they're often part and parcel of a kundalini awakening, especially when it goes sideways into a crisis. It's not a sign you're crazy, though it sure feels like it. It's a sign that something big is shifting inside you, something energetic, something ancient. And sometimes, when that energy starts moving, it kicks up a lot of dust. Not just internal dust, but external dust, too. Or what seems external, anyway. You're seeing stuff that most people don't. And it's intense. Right?

I'm not going to sugarcoat this. It's a rough ride. But you can get through it. You can learn to work with what's happening, instead of being completely overwhelmed by it. That's what I had to do. That's what many of us have had to do. So let's talk about it, raw and real. No bullshit, just what I've learned from the trenches.

What You're Actually Seeing - Or Feeling

First off, let's get clear on what "entities" might mean in this context. It's not always a classic ghost or demon, though it can feel that way. It can be:

  • Shadow figures: These are common. You catch them out of the corner of your eye, or they seem to coalesce in a dark corner. They might feel menacing, or just unsettling.
  • Figments of light/energy: Sometimes it's not a solid form, but a swirling mass of light, or a pulsating energy field. These can be beautiful or equally frightening if you don't know what you're looking at.
  • Archetypal figures: You might see deities, angels, demons, mythical creatures - things from collective consciousness or your own subconscious. They can be incredibly vivid, almost hyper-real.
  • Deceased loved ones or strangers: Sometimes people report seeing spirits of the dead. This can be comforting or deeply disturbing, depending on the context.
  • "Presences" or "feelings": You might not see anything concrete, but you feel something there. A weight, a cold spot, a sense of being watched, an undeniable feeling of another intelligence in the room. This is just as valid and often just as scary as seeing something.
  • Auditory phenomena: Whispers, voices, music, strange noises that aren't there. This goes hand-in-hand with visual experiences for many.

Know what I mean? It's a whole spectrum of weirdness. And your mind, which is already under immense stress from the kundalini energy, is trying to make sense of it all. It's grasping for explanations, and often, the only explanations it can find are terrifying ones because these experiences don't fit into our everyday reality.

Why Are You Seeing This Stuff?

This is the million-dollar question, isn't it? And honestly, there's no single, universally accepted answer. But from my experience, and from talking to countless others who've been through this, here are some possibilities:

Increased Psychic Sensitivity

Kundalini energy is about awakening. It's about opening up to more than just the physical world. When it moves, it often rips open your psychic senses. It's like you've been living with blinders on, and suddenly they're ripped off. You're seeing, hearing, feeling things that were always there, but you weren't tuned into them. It's like turning up the volume on a radio that was always playing. The problem is, you don't have a filter yet. You're getting everything, all at once, without a way to sort it or turn it down. This can be incredibly disorienting. It's not that entities are suddenly appearing; it's that you're suddenly aware of them. Does that land?

Release of Stored Trauma and Energy

Kundalini is a cleansing fire. It burns through blockages, old traumas, suppressed emotions. Sometimes, these "blockages" aren't just feelings; they're energetic imprints, or even what some call "attachments." When this energy starts to move, it can dislodge these things. They might manifest as external entities, projections of your own inner demons or fears, or actual energetic hangers-on that are being dislodged from your field. It's like a spiritual detox, and sometimes the toxins look like monsters on the way out.

The Veil Thins

For some, the kundalini process thins the veil between dimensions. It's not just your personal energy field being cleansed, but your perception of reality itself expanding. You're dipping into other realms, other frequencies. And in those realms, there are all sorts of beings. Some are benevolent, some are neutral, and some... well, some aren't so nice. You're getting a glimpse behind the curtain, and it's not always pretty. This can feel like you're going insane because your brain is struggling to process information that doesn't fit its established rules.

What to Do When They Appear - Practical Survival

Okay, so you know why it might be happening. Now, what do you DO? Because knowing the "why" doesn't make the shadows in your room any less real or terrifying. Here's what I learned, often the hard way.

Don't Engage (Initially)

This is crucial. Your first instinct might be to fight, to scream, to run. Or to try and talk to them. Don't. Not at first. If you're scared, if you're in crisis, you're not in a good place to engage. Engaging can give them more energy, more focus. It can make them stronger, or seem stronger. For now, your goal is self-preservation. You're in survival mode. Think of it like this: if you were drowning, you wouldn't try to have a conversation with the water, right? You'd try to get to shore. Your shore is stability and safety.

What I mean by "don't engage" is: don't feed them your fear. Don't stare at them. Don't talk to them. Don't acknowledge them beyond a firm, internal "I see you, but you don't belong here, and I'm not giving you my energy."

Ground Yourself, Immediately

This is your number one tool. When you're seeing things, you're usually ungrounded, your energy is scattered, your nervous system is in overdrive. You need to pull yourself back into your body, into the present moment, into the physical world. How?

  • Physical sensations: Feel your feet on the floor. Wiggle your toes. Touch something real - a blanket, your arm, the wall. Focus intensely on the texture.
  • Breathing: Slow, deep breaths. In through the nose, out through the mouth. Count them. Focus on the sensation of air filling and leaving your lungs. This calms your nervous system.
  • Nature: If you can, go outside. Put your bare feet on the grass or dirt. Lean against a tree. Feel the wind on your face. Nature is incredibly grounding.
  • Eat something substantial: A piece of toast, some fruit, a warm drink. Something that makes you feel solid and connected to the physical.

The more grounded you are, the less susceptible you are to these phenomena. They thrive on fear and ungrounded energy. You're basically making yourself a less appealing target.

Set Clear Boundaries

Even if you don't engage, you need to set firm boundaries. Internally, and sometimes verbally. State it clearly: "This is my space. You are not welcome here. I command you to leave." You don't have to shout it, but you need to mean it with every fiber of your being. Project that intention. Imagine a shield of white light around you, around your home. Visualize it. Feel it. It's not about fighting; it's about asserting your sovereignty. This is YOUR energy field, YOUR space.

I know this sounds crazy, like something out of a movie. But when you're in this state, your intentions have power. Your will has power. You just have to remember you have it.

Protect Your Space

This isn't just internal. You can use physical tools to help you feel safer and create a more protective environment:

  • Salt: A line of salt across doorways and windowsills is an old-school protective measure. You don't have to believe in magic for it to work psychologically, and sometimes that's enough.
  • Sage or Palo Santo: Smudge your space. Clear out old, stagnant energy. Again, the ritual itself can be powerfully calming and empowering.
  • Light: Keep lights on if you need to. Don't sit in the dark if it makes you feel vulnerable.
  • Sound: Play calming music, or even just some white noise. Silence can sometimes feel oppressive when you're sensitive.
  • Crystals: Black tourmaline, obsidian, amethyst - many people find these crystals help with protection and grounding. Hold one, keep it by your bed.

These aren't foolproof magical wards, but they help you feel safer, and that feeling of safety is critical when you're in crisis. You're tricking your nervous system into calming down, and that's a good thing.

Seeking Support - You Can't Do This Alone

This is not a solo journey. You need help. And finding the right kind of help is critical. Most mainstream doctors won't understand. They'll likely try to medicate away the symptoms, which might help you sleep, but won't address the underlying energetic process. You need people who understand spiritual emergency.

  • Spiritual Emergency Resources: There are organizations and individuals who specialize in this. Look for resources on "spiritual emergence" or "spiritual crisis." Books like Spiritual Emergency: When Personal Transformation Becomes a Crisis (paid link) are lifelines. They'll make you feel less alone, more understood.
  • Kundalini-Aware Therapists/Coaches: Some therapists are trained in transpersonal psychology or have personal experience with kundalini. They can help you integrate these experiences without pathologizing them. A Sourcebook for Helping People with Spiritual Problems by Emma Bragdon (paid link) lists resources for finding such practitioners.
  • Trusted Friends/Family: If you have someone who is open-minded and you trust deeply, talk to them. Even if they don't understand, having someone listen without judgment can be incredibly healing.
  • Community: Online forums, support groups - find others who've been through this. There's immense comfort in knowing you're not the only one. You'll find people who get it, who've seen their own shadows and emerged.

I can't stress this enough: you need to talk about it. Keeping it all inside will only make it worse. Find safe spaces to share your experiences. Someone who has been there, done that, can tell you, "Yeah, that's normal for this process," and that alone can bring immense relief.

Long-Term Strategies for Integration

Once the initial terror subsides a bit, and you've found some grounding, you'll need to start thinking about how to live with this heightened sensitivity. Because truth is, it might not just go away. It might become a part of who you are now.

  • Daily Grounding Practices: Make grounding a non-negotiable part of your day. Meditation, yoga, spending time in nature, mindful eating. These aren't luxuries; they're necessities.
  • Energy Hygiene: Just like you shower daily, you need to cleanse your energy field daily. Visualization of white light, sound baths, salt baths - find what works for you to clear out anything you might have picked up.
  • Discernment: As you get stronger, you'll learn to discern what's real, what's a projection of your own fear, and what's just background noise. Not everything you see or feel is significant. Learn to trust your gut. If it feels bad, it probably is, and you can tell it to leave. If it feels neutral or benevolent, you might eventually explore it, but only when you're strong and stable.
  • Study Kundalini: Knowledge is power. Understanding the process can demystify it and reduce fear. Books like The Kundalini Experience by Lee Sannella (paid link) and The Kundalini Guide by Bonnie Greenwell (paid link) can provide context and reassurance.
  • Build a Strong Inner Core: This process forces you to confront everything within yourself. Use it to build resilience, self-love, and inner strength. The stronger you are internally, the less external phenomena can shake you.

Here's the thing: you're not going crazy. You're going through something incredibly intense, something that pushes the boundaries of normal human experience. It's messy, it's scary, and it feels like it will never end. But it does get better. You learn to manage it. You learn to integrate it. You learn to live with this expanded awareness. And eventually, you might even see it as a gift, though I know that's a hard sell right now when you're staring down a shadow in your room.

So yeah, take a deep breath. You're still here. You're surviving. And you will continue to survive. You've got this, even if it doesn't feel like it. Just keep grounding, keep setting boundaries, and keep reaching out for help. You're not alone in the dark.

This is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If you're in crisis, please reach out to a qualified therapist or call 988.
author: Kalesh
